description: Professional 6-page static website for Omega Construction — a Virginia-based residential and commercial general contractor. Built with plain HTML, Tailwind CSS (CDN), and vanilla JS. Deployable to GitHub Pages with zero build tooling.

userStories.title: Project scaffold & shared assets
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the file structure, Tailwind config, Google Fonts, shared CSS, and shared JS in place so every subsequent story has a consistent foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory structure exists: assets/css/, assets/js/, assets/images/placeholder/
assets/css/custom.css created with: html { scroll-behavior: smooth }, hero overlay utility class, transition defaults
assets/js/main.js created with: mobile nav toggle, scroll-based nav background switch, smooth-scroll anchor handler
README.md documents GitHub Pages deployment steps and instructs developer to replace YOUR_FORM_ID in contact.html with a real Formspree endpoint
Tailwind config block (inline in a script tag) defines brand colors: navy #1A2E4A, amber #C8963E, cream #F7F4EF, slate #64748B and font families: Playfair Display (serif) and Inter (sans)
Google Fonts link tag loads Playfair Display and Inter with display=swap
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Shared nav & footer HTML partials documented
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the exact nav and footer HTML written out once (in a comment block or standalone reference file) so I can copy-paste them consistently into every page without drift.
acceptanceCriteria: index.html created at repository root containing the full shared <head> block (charset, viewport, title, Google Fonts, Tailwind CDN script with config, custom.css link)

Nav markup inside index.html: fixed top, navy bg, Ω OMEGA CONSTRUCTION logo with amber omega glyph, links (Home / About / Services / Projects / Contact), amber 'Get a Quote' CTA button, hamburger button for mobile
Footer markup inside index.html: navy bg, 3-column layout (logo+tagline / quick links / contact details), copyright line '© 2025 Omega Construction LLC. All rights reserved.'
Nav hamburger toggles mobile menu open/closed via main.js (no CSS-only hack)
Nav background switches from transparent to solid navy when user scrolls past 50px
Active page link on Home is highlighted with amber color or underline
All nav and footer links use relative paths (e.g., ./about.html not /about.html)
Footer is responsive: 3 columns on desktop, stacked single column on mobile
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Home page — Hero & Trust Bar sections
userStories.description: As a first-time visitor, I want to land on a strong, full-screen hero with a clear headline and two CTA buttons, followed by a trust bar showing key stats, so I immediately understand who Omega is and feel confident.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section is full viewport height (min-h-screen) with a placeholder background image (1920x1080, placehold.co brand colors) and a navy overlay at 60% opacity
Hero headline: 'Built to Last. Built for You.' in Playfair Display, white, large (text-5xl md:text-7xl)
Hero sub-headline: 'Omega Construction delivers residential and commercial projects across Virginia — on time, on budget, and built right.' in Inter, white/80 opacity
Two CTA buttons: 'View Our Work' links to ./projects.html, 'Get a Free Quote' links to ./contact.html
Animated scroll-down chevron at hero bottom center
Trust bar below hero: cream background, 4 stat blocks (25+ Years Experience / 300+ Projects Completed / Residential & Commercial / Licensed & Insured), amber number + slate label
Trust bar is responsive: 4 columns on desktop, 2x2 on tablet, 2x2 on mobile
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Home page — Services Overview & Featured Projects sections
userStories.description: As a first-time visitor, I want to see what Omega offers and get a glimpse of their portfolio so I can quickly decide if they match my project needs.
acceptanceCriteria: Services Overview section: amber overline 'What We Do', heading 'End-to-End Construction Services', 3-column card grid (New Construction / Renovation & Remodeling / Commercial Build-Out)
Each service card has: inline SVG icon, title, 2-sentence description, amber 'Learn More →' link to ./services.html
Card grid collapses to 1 column on mobile
Featured Projects section: amber overline 'Our Work', heading 'Projects We're Proud Of', 3 project cards with 1200x800 placeholder images, project name, Residential/Commercial tag
Each project card has a 'View Project' link to ./projects.html
Amber 'See All Projects →' text link below project grid links to ./projects.html
Both sections use max-w-7xl mx-auto px-6 and py-20 vertical padding
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Home page — Why Choose Omega, Testimonials & CTA Banner sections
userStories.description: As a first-time visitor, I want to read about Omega's differentiators and real client feedback, then be prompted to take action, so I feel ready to reach out.
acceptanceCriteria: Why Choose Omega section: 2-column layout (placeholder image left, text right), heading 'Your Project. Our Commitment.', 4 checkmark bullet points (Transparent Pricing / Dedicated Project Manager / Clean Jobsites / Warranty on Workmanship), 'About Our Team' button links to ./about.html
Two-column layout stacks to single column on mobile (image on top)
Testimonials section: cream background, 3 cards with placeholder quote text, client name, and project type label
CTA Banner: navy background, amber headline 'Ready to Start Your Project?', body copy, 'Call (703) 555-0192' button uses tel: link, 'Send Us a Message' button links to ./contact.html
Home page has no horizontal scrollbar at 375px viewport width
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: About page — full build
userStories.description: As a potential client, I want to learn about Omega's history, values, team, and credentials so I can decide if I trust them with my project.
acceptanceCriteria: about.html created at repository root with full shared head, nav (active link = About), and footer
Page hero: 50vh height, placeholder background image, breadcrumb 'Home > About', heading 'Who We Are'
Company Story section: 2-column (text left, placeholder image right), heading 'Built on Integrity, Driven by Craft', 3 placeholder paragraphs in conversational first-person plural tone
Core Values section: 4-card grid (Integrity / Quality / Communication / Safety), each with SVG icon and 1-sentence explanation; 4 columns on desktop, 2x2 on mobile
Meet the Team section: 3-person grid with 600x600 placeholder portrait images, name, title, 2-sentence bio (roles: Founder & Principal / Project Manager / Site Superintendent)
Certifications strip: 4 placeholder affiliation blocks in a horizontal row (Virginia Class A Contractor License / OSHA 30 Certified / NAHB Member / BBB Accredited)
CTA Banner (same as home) at bottom
All 2-column layouts collapse to single column on mobile
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Services page — service blocks & process timeline
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to read detailed descriptions of each service Omega offers and understand their process step-by-step so I can confirm they handle my project type.
acceptanceCriteria: services.html created at repository root with full shared head, nav (active link = Services), and footer
Page hero: 50vh, heading 'What We Build', subheadline about full-service construction
4 service blocks with alternating image/text layout (img-left/text-right then flip): Custom Home Construction / Renovation & Remodeling / Commercial Construction / Project Management & Consulting
Each block has: 1200x800 placeholder image, heading, 2-paragraph description, bullet list of 4 specifics
Alternating layout collapses to stacked (image on top, text below) on mobile
Process timeline: heading 'How We Work', 5 steps (Discovery Call / Site Assessment / Detailed Estimate / Build Phase / Final Walkthrough)
Each step has amber numbered circle, step name, 1-sentence description
Timeline is horizontal on desktop (flex row), vertical on mobile (flex column with left-aligned numbers)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Services page — FAQ accordion
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to expand and collapse common questions about Omega's services so I can get quick answers without leaving the page.
acceptanceCriteria: FAQ section added to services.html below the process timeline, heading 'Common Questions'
6 Q&A items covering: licensing, service area, timeline estimates, payment terms, subcontractors, permits
Each item: question row (clickable, amber chevron icon), answer panel (hidden by default)
Clicking a question expands its answer and collapses any previously open answer (only one open at a time)
Chevron rotates 180° when item is open (CSS transform transition)
Accordion logic implemented in main.js using data attributes and classList toggle
CTA Banner added after FAQ section
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Projects page — grid & filter
userStories.description: As a potential client, I want to browse Omega's portfolio and filter by project type so I can find examples relevant to my own project.
acceptanceCriteria: projects.html created at repository root with full shared head, nav (active link = Projects), and footer
Page hero: 50vh, heading 'Our Work', subheadline
Filter bar: 3 toggle buttons (All / Residential / Commercial), amber underline/fill on active button, outlined style on inactive
9 project cards in a 3-column grid (2-col on tablet, 1-col on mobile): 5 residential + 4 commercial
Each card: 1200x800 placeholder image, project name (e.g. 'Fairfax Custom Home'), type tag, hover overlay with brief description and 'View Details' button
Clicking a filter button shows/hides cards by toggling CSS 'hidden' class based on data-type attribute — no page reload
Active filter state updates correctly when switching between All / Residential / Commercial
Grid layout does not break or leave gaps when some cards are hidden
CTA Banner at bottom
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Projects page — lightbox
userStories.description: As a potential client, I want to click a project card and see an enlarged image with project details in an overlay so I can review the work without leaving the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'View Details' on any project card opens a full-screen lightbox overlay
Lightbox shows: large version of project image, project name, location, scope (1-sentence), completion year
Lightbox has an X close button in the top-right corner
Clicking outside the lightbox panel closes it
Pressing Escape key closes the lightbox
Lightbox open/close implemented in main.js with no external library
Body scroll is locked (overflow: hidden) while lightbox is open
Lightbox is fully usable on mobile (image scales to fit screen width)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Contact page — full build
userStories.description: As a ready-to-hire visitor, I want to easily reach Omega by phone or inquiry form, with zero friction, so I can start my project without confusion.
acceptanceCriteria: contact.html created at repository root with full shared head, nav (active link = Contact), and footer
Page hero: 40vh, heading 'Let's Talk About Your Project', friendly subheadline
Left column (40% desktop): phone (703) 555-0192 as large tel: link, email info@omegaconstruction.com as mailto: link, office hours Mon-Fri 7am-5pm, service area text, static placeholder map image

Right column (60% desktop): inquiry form with fields: Full Name (required), Email (required), Phone, Project Type dropdown (Residential / Commercial / Not Sure), Project Description textarea (required), Preferred Contact Method radio (Phone / Email)
Form submit button: amber, text 'Send My Inquiry', full-width on mobile
Form action='https://formspree.io/f/YOUR_FORM_ID' method='POST' with YOUR_FORM_ID clearly marked as a placeholder
Client-side JS validation in main.js: submitting with empty required fields (Name, Email, Description) shows inline red error messages and prevents submission
On successful Formspree submission, form is replaced with a thank-you message: 'Thank you! We'll be in touch within 1 business day.'
Two-column layout stacks to single column on mobile (contact info on top, form below)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: 404 page
userStories.description: As a lost visitor, I want a friendly error page with clear navigation options so I'm not stranded and can easily return to the site.
acceptanceCriteria: 404.html created at repository root with full shared head, nav, and footer
Page title in browser tab: '404 — Page Not Found | Omega Construction'
Centered content area: large '404' text in amber (text-9xl), heading 'Page Not Found' in navy Playfair Display, friendly body copy explaining the page doesn't exist
'Go Home' button links to ./index.html
'Contact Us' button links to ./contact.html
Page renders correctly at 375px mobile width
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
